name: Everett & Jones BBQ
description: An Oakland institution since 1973. Slow-smoked ribs, links, and chicken with sauce that hits different. A true legacy Black-owned spot passed down through three generations of the Jones family.

name: Nyum Bai
description: James Beard Award-nominated Cambodian restaurant celebrating the food of Phnom Penh. Nite Yun's cooking is deeply personal — every dish is a story about home, memory, and survival.

name: Marcus Books
description: The oldest Black-owned bookstore in the US, open since 1960. A cultural landmark, community gathering place, and living archive of Black thought. More than a bookstore — a movement.

name: Souley Vegan
description: Oakland's pioneering Black-owned vegan restaurant. Soul food reimagined — collard greens, mac and cheese, and fried everything, without the meat. A community favorite for over 15 years.

name: Minnie Bell's Soul Movement
description: Chef Fernay McPherson's restaurant is a love letter to her grandmother's kitchen. From the crispy chicken to the from-scratch mac and cheese, every dish tells a story about Black culinary tradition.

name: Hawker Fare
description: Chef James Syhabout's love letter to Southeast Asian street food. Filipino and Isan Thai flavors collide in a vibrant, loud, and deeply delicious setting that feels like eating at a night market.

name: Farmhouse Kitchen Thai Cuisine
description: Authentic Thai recipes passed through generations, brought to the Bay by a family from Chiang Mai. The Pad Thai here will ruin you for every other version you've ever had.

name: Daughter Thai Kitchen & Bar
description: Modern Thai cuisine with a cocktail program to match. The kind of spot where you go for dinner and stay for the night. Vibrant, creative, and always packed for a reason.

name: Pican
description: Upscale Southern cuisine in the heart of Oakland. Refined takes on the classics — fried chicken, collard greens, and biscuits — in an elegant setting that never forgets its roots.

name: Burma Superstar
description: The restaurant that introduced the Bay Area to Burmese cuisine. The Tea Leaf Salad alone is a cultural experience. Expect a wait — it is absolutely worth every minute.

name: Bica Coffeehouse
description: A cozy, community-rooted coffee shop in the heart of Oakland. Carefully sourced beans, a welcoming vibe, and a space where the community actually feels at home.

name: Asha Tea House
description: Elevated Asian tea culture meets California creativity. From hand-pulled boba to rare oolongs, this is a tea experience unlike anything you've had. The sesame soft serve is non-negotiable.

name: Authentic Bagel Company
description: Hand-rolled, kettle-boiled bagels crafted by a Black-owned Oakland bakery. Rotating flavors, creative schmear options, and genuine neighborhood energy every morning.

name: Reem's California
description: James Beard-nominated Arab-American bakery and restaurant. Freshly baked mana'eesh, aromatic coffee, and a space that centers immigrant foodways with pride and joy.

name: Perle Wine Bar
description: A sophisticated, welcoming wine bar owned and operated by a Black woman sommelier. Natural wines, small bites, and a space designed so everyone feels seen — and pours generously.

name: Yoshi's Oakland
description: One of the country's most beloved jazz clubs. World-class musicians, Japanese-influenced cuisine, and a room that feels like history is happening every single night.

name: Oaklandish
description: The original Oakland pride brand. Employee-owned and deeply rooted in the community — every purchase supports local artists and community organizations fighting for the soul of Oakland.

name: The Hair Bar Oakland
description: A natural hair salon celebrating Black hair in all its textures. Expert locticians and stylists who actually know what they are doing with coils, curls, and kinks. No relaxers, no judgment.

name: Destiny Arts Center
description: Oakland's premier youth arts organization and performance space. Dance, martial arts, theater — all centering the brilliance of BIPOC young people. Check their events calendar before any Bay visit.

name: Taqueria El Farolito
description: A Mission District legend open until 3am. Cash-only, no frills, just the best carne asada burrito in San Francisco. The line always moves fast and it is always worth it.

name: Nopalito
description: Handmade, organic Mexican food rooted in the culinary traditions of Michoacán and Oaxaca. The tamales alone are worth the trip across the bridge. Warm, community-driven, and deeply delicious.

name: Shizen Vegan Sushi Bar & Izakaya
description: Entirely vegan Japanese cuisine that will make you forget anything else existed. Creative rolls, house-made broths, and an izakaya energy that is loud, fun, and impossible to replicate.

name: Pakwan Restaurant
description: A SF institution for halal Pakistani and Indian food. Enormous portions, impossibly reasonable prices, and a line that moves fast. Pure, unpretentious, unforgettable flavor.

name: Precita Eyes Muralists
description: The organization behind the Mission's iconic murals. Take a guided mural tour or visit the gallery. A living piece of Latinx cultural history painted directly onto the walls of San Francisco.

name: Auntie April's Chicken, Waffles & Such
description: A Bayview institution and Black-owned neighborhood anchor. The chicken and waffles are legendary, but it's the love baked into every dish that keeps the community coming back for decades.

name: Angeline's Louisiana Kitchen
description: Bayou cooking in the East Bay. Crawfish étouffée, gumbo, and fried catfish made with the kind of care that only comes from genuine love for the cuisine and the culture it comes from.

name: Imm Thai Street Food
description: Casual, affordable, and deeply authentic Thai street food in Berkeley. The kind of place you discover once and immediately start bringing everyone you know. A neighborhood gem in every sense.

ownership.name: Lahore Karahi
ownership.description: No-frills, maximum flavor Pakistani cooking in Fremont. The karahi here is some of the best in the entire Bay Area — rich, aromatic, generous portions, and outstanding value.

ownership.name: Rooh SF
ownership.description: Modern progressive Indian cuisine that reimagines the subcontinent's flavors with California sensibility. This is Indian food for people who think they already know Indian food. It will change your mind.

ownership.name: Nailheads
ownership.description: An artsy, community-forward nail studio in Oakland that is anything but the average salon. Intricate designs, quality non-toxic products, and a genuinely relaxed and inclusive atmosphere.
